---
title: "Home"
author: "Assa Yeroslaviz"
date: '**last update** - `r format(Sys.time(), "%d %B, %Y")`'
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

This website documents the BCF NGS mapping automation workflow.
It is designed for reproducible, fast, and consistent preliminary analysis of sequencing projects at the core facility.

## Why this tool exists

The same mapping tasks are repeated across many projects: environment setup, genome preparation, and standardized alignment/output generation.
This tool provides one consistent interface so these steps are less error-prone, easier to review, and easier to run on local machines or HPC.

## What the workflow can do

- Build and maintain a dedicated micromamba environment for the pipeline.
- Check whether a newer Ensembl release is available.
- Download FASTA/GTF files and build versioned genome indices.
- Build indices for multiple aligners (`STAR`, `bwa`, `bowtie2`).
- Run preflight validation (`lint`, config checks, dry-run) before real execution.
- Map single-end or paired-end FASTQ data to a selected organism.
- Produce mapping outputs and normalized `bigWig` tracks for visualization.
- Run with local execution or SLURM execution.

## Main entry point: `scripts/bcfngs`

The main command-line wrapper is `bcfngs` (`scripts/bcfngs`).
It is the recommended way to run the workflow end-to-end because it centralizes checks, planning, and execution.

Available command groups:

- `bcfngs env ...` for environment management.
- `bcfngs genomes ...` for genome download/index lifecycle.
- `bcfngs mapping ...` for project mapping runs.


## Available steps in `bcfngs`

1. Environment setup and validation
   - `bcfngs env create`
   - `bcfngs env update`
   - `bcfngs env check`
2. Genome maintenance
   - `bcfngs genomes check-updates`
   - `bcfngs genomes plan`
   - `bcfngs genomes build`
3. Mapping workflow
   - `bcfngs mapping plan` (dry-run + checks)
   - `bcfngs mapping run` (real run)

## Short usage summary

```bash
# 1) create/update and validate environment
./scripts/bcfngs env create
./scripts/bcfngs env check

# 2) (optional) check for newer Ensembl release
./scripts/bcfngs genomes check-updates

# 3) plan and build genomes/indices
./scripts/bcfngs genomes plan
./scripts/bcfngs genomes build

# 4) plan and run mapping
./scripts/bcfngs mapping plan --paired-end --mapper star
./scripts/bcfngs mapping run  --paired-end --mapper star
```

Configuration is split into:

- `configs/genomes.yaml` for organisms, releases, and index settings.
- `configs/run.yaml` for per-project mapping settings (project, input path, organism, output path).

## Detailed guides

This page is an overview. Full details are documented in the dedicated sections:

- [Environment setup](environment.html)
- [Snakemake workflow details](snakemake.html)
- [Updating genomes](updating_genomes.html)
- [Legacy quick HOWTO](HOWTO.html)


# Available Organisms

To keep the list short and focus on the organisms used most often, the tool contains the following organisms:

1. human
2. mouse
3. zebrafish
4. fruit fly
5. yeast
6. worm (C. elegans)
7. Chicken

as well as the following mappers:

1. star
2. bwa
3. bowtie2

For any other tools or organisms, please don't hesitate to contact me.

# CHANGES

### 04.03.2026

- Improved `./scripts/bcfngs genomes plan` preflight output:
  - Replaced the generic `OK` with a clear config validation message (`Config file is OK: ...`).
  - Replaced raw `HTTP/1.1 200 OK` header output from URL checks with readable per-organism status lines for FASTA and GTF URLs.
- Added version reporting in `genomes plan`:
  - Prints pinned genome build release(s), including Ensembl (currently 115 from config).
  - Prints configured tool versions for STAR, bwa, and bowtie2.
- Added Snakemake per-rule logging and environment declarations in `workflow/genomes.smk`:
  - Added `log:` targets for `download_sources`, `star_index`, `bwa_index`, `bowtie2_index`, and `chrom_size`.
  - Redirected rule command output to the corresponding log files.
  - Added `conda: "../env/environment.yml"` to those rules.
- Fixed follow-up issues introduced during logging changes:
  - Updated `download_sources` log path to include `{src_rel}` so output and log wildcards match.
  - Escaped shell block braces in `shell:` commands (`{{ ... }}`) to avoid Snakemake formatting errors.
- Added unified mapping workflow (`workflow/mapping.smk`) with STAR, bwa, and bowtie2 support.
- Added wrapper commands:
  - `./scripts/bcfngs mapping plan`
  - `./scripts/bcfngs mapping run`
- Added paired-end/single-end mode as CLI flags (paired-end default).
- Replaced `bam2wig.py` conversion with `deeptools bamCoverage`.
- Added FASTQ pattern auto-detection for multiple naming schemes.
- Archived old STAR-only mapping Snakefiles under `archive/legacy/mapping/`.
- Added dedicated mapping documentation page (`mapping.qmd`).
- Documented the default cluster micromamba environment path consistently across docs:
  - `/fs/gpfs41/lv07/fileset07/home/b_cox/yeroslaviz/miniconda3/envs/update_genomes`
- Updated environment/setup command examples in:
  - [`environment`](environment.html)
  - [`snakemake`](snakemake.html)
  - [`Updating genomes`](updating_genomes.html)

### 20.02.2026

- Added Quarto website configuration (`_quarto.yml`) and re-rendered site with `quarto render`.
- Updated navbar:
  - Added links to [`environment`](environment.html), [`snakemake`](snakemake.html), and [`Updating genomes`](updating_genomes.html).
  - Replaced the old contact page with an email link: <mailto:yeroslaviz@biochem.mpg.de>.
  - Removed Twitter and added Bluesky.
- Converted `scripts/Updating_genomes.txt` into [`updating_genomes.qmd`](updating_genomes.qmd) (and removed the original text file).
- Switched environment management docs from conda to micromamba:
  - Added [`environment.qmd`](environment.qmd) and [`env/environment.yml`](env/environment.yml).
  - Added cookbook-style “how-to” instructions for common scenarios (new installs, updates, HPC module fallback).
- Added a new “one command” wrapper CLI: [`scripts/bcfngs`](scripts/bcfngs) (preflight checks, lint, dry-run, then run).
- Added new Snakemake configuration + workflow for genome download and indexing:
  - [`configs/genomes.yaml`](configs/genomes.yaml) (scientific organism IDs, pinned releases, index versioning)
  - [`configs/run.yaml`](configs/run.yaml) (per-project mapping settings)
  - [`workflow/genomes.smk`](workflow/genomes.smk) (downloads FASTA/GTF, builds STAR+bwa+bowtie2 indices, updates symlinks, creates chromSize)
- Updated the STAR mapping Snakefiles to use `configs/run.yaml` and scientific organism IDs.

### 27.06.2022

new genome builds were added to the workflow

- _D. melanogaster_ - `BDGP6.28`
- _E. coli_ - `HUSEC2011CHR1`
- _D. pseudoobscura_ - `3.0.49`
- _C. elegans_ - `WBcel 2.35`

### 13.01.2020

- a short [`HOWTO`](HOWTO.html) was added. This serves as a fast introduction, if `conda` and `snakemake` are already installed and set for the mapping. 
- the genome of _D. pseudoobscura_ was added to the `config.yaml` file.

### 11.12.2019

The workflow now should work on the HPC cluster of the MPI. For that reason some changes were made to the folder structure and the depth of the analysis.

1. the mappers `bowtie2` and `bwa` were removed from the analysis. It will be done from now on only with `STAR`.
2. the working directory of the analysis should be the `conc.fastq` folder in the pool-bcfngs pool folder of the Sequencing core facility.
3. The workflow will create a new folder with the structure `ProjectNumber/Organism/` where it will save two new folders for the star mapping results and the bigwig files.
4. the first script to get the genomes should be only run once, or when the genome version must be updated.
5. The second script was split into single-end and paired-end versions and should be run accordingly.
